title: Rich Light Spanish Beef Toast
description: A flavorful yet light Spanish toast featuring a quick-seared feather blade steak, toasted bread, tomato jam, and fresh accents

ingredients.name: Saffron threads
ingredients.notes: optional, steeped in a splash of water

instructions: Bring the beef to room temperature for 10–15 minutes and pat dry with paper towels.
Season both sides of the steak with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, and saffron (if using).
Heat a cast‑iron skillet over high heat and add 1 tbsp olive oil. When the oil shimmers, place the steak in the skillet.
Sear for about 2–3 minutes per side until a nice crust forms and the internal temperature reaches 55 °C (131 °F) for medium‑rare.
Transfer the steak to a plate and let it rest for 5 minutes. Slice thinly against the grain.
While the steak rests, toast the bread slices in a toaster or in a pan with a drizzle of extra‑virgin olive oil until golden brown.
Spread a thin layer of roasted tomato jam on each toasted slice.
Place the sliced steak on top of the jam.
Drizzle fresh lemon juice and a small amount of extra‑virgin olive oil over the steak.
Sprinkle chopped parsley, sea salt, and a little fresh pepper for finishing.
Serve immediately while the toast is still warm and the steak is juicy.
